political.s: Organic Valley reports minimal direct federal lobbying ($0 in 2024) and limited PAC contributions ($7,753 in 2024 cycle). The cooperative participates through industry associations but maintains relatively low political engagement compared to conventional dairy operations.

charity.s: Organic Valley demonstrates strong charitable commitment through multiple initiatives including food donations to food banks, farmer-funded grant program (FAFO) supporting organic research and education, and community partnerships. Donated over 140,000 pounds of food during COVID-19 and awards grants supporting organic agriculture advancement.
charity.amt: Over $2 million annually (2008), ongoing multi-million dollar FAFO grants
charity.focus: Organic research, education, family farming support, food insecurity, environmental preservation, humane animal treatment

environment.s: Organic Valley demonstrates excellent environmental performance through carbon-neutral dairy production 24% lower emissions than conventional farms, regenerative agriculture practices, prevention of 540+ million pounds of agrichemicals since 1988, and comprehensive Carbon Insetting Program. Recently received $25 million USDA grant for climate-smart practices.

labor.s: Limited specific OSHA/NLRB data available for Organic Valley directly, but member farms follow USDA organic standards requiring quality animal care and outdoor access. Glassdoor compensation ratings average 3.6/5 stars; salary ranges suggest wage inequality from $44,016 entry-level to $272,991 executive positions with average employee satisfaction moderate.

dei.s: Limited public information on formal DEI programs or commitments. Company appointed first female CEO in 2025, indicating some organizational change, but specific diversity metrics and anti-DEI stances are not prominently documented.

animals.s: Organic Valley emphasizes humane animal treatment through USDA organic compliance requiring organic feed, pasture access during grazing season, and preventative animal care standards. Marketing emphasizes quality animal care as fundamental to production values and product quality.

guns.s: No evidence of gun sales, manufacturing, or involvement with firearms industry identified in company operations.

privacy.s: Limited public transparency regarding data privacy policies and practices. As a consumer-facing cooperative, privacy practices appear mixed with standard e-commerce and newsletter data collection but insufficient disclosure of comprehensive privacy standards.

execPay.s: Executive compensation shows significant disparity with Vice President salaries reaching $272,991 annually versus entry-level positions at $44,016 annually, representing a 6.2:1 ratio. No formal pay equity disclosure or CEO-to-worker ratio transparency identified.
